join_order.build: num(uom=pure, value IS NOT NULL) → GROUP BY (adsh_code, tag_code) → MAX(value)
join_order.notes: Phase 1: zone-map-guided scan of ~2 blocks builds hash-agg map {adsh_code,tag_code}->max_value (~118k entries, ~2MB, fits L3 cache). Phase 2: identical zone-map scan probes map; keeps rows where value == max_value (double equality).
adsh_code == sub row_id by encoding contract; check sub/fy.bin[adsh_code] == (int16_t)2022 as O(1) direct array index. No hash table needed. Selectivity ~21% reduces survivors to ~24.8k rows.

filters.notes: pure_code resolved at runtime from shared/uom.dict; expected value 2 but MUST load from dict
applied as direct_array lookup sub/fy.bin[adsh_code] during Phase 2 probe

data_structures.uom_dict: load shared/uom.dict at startup; scan entries to find code for string 'pure'; store as pure_code (int8_t)
data_structures.m_hash_agg: compact_hash_map pre_sized to 131072 buckets; key={adsh_code:int32, tag_code:int32} (8 bytes); value=max_value:double (8 bytes); ~2MB total; fits L3 cache; built in Phase 1, probed in Phase 2
data_structures.sub_fy_array: mmap sub/fy.bin as int16_t[86135]; O(1) lookup by adsh_code
data_structures.sub_name_array: mmap sub/name.bin as int32_t[86135] + load sub/name.dict; decode name string at final output stage only
data_structures.tag_dict: load shared/tag_numpre.dict; decode tag string at final output stage only
data_structures.output_heap: top-k heap (k=100) keyed by (value DESC, name ASC, tag ASC); populated during Phase 2 after all filters pass

scan_phases.name: build_m
scan_phases.block_selection: num_zonemaps: keep blocks where uom_min <= pure_code <= uom_max; expected ~2 blocks of 100k rows each

scan_phases.name: probe_m_and_sub
scan_phases.row_filter: uom_code == pure_code && !isnan(value) && m_hash_agg.lookup({adsh_code, tag_code}) == value && sub/fy.bin[adsh_code] == (int16_t)2022
scan_phases.action: insert surviving (adsh_code, tag_code, value) into top-100 output_heap

parallelism.notes: Zone-map skip limits scan to ~2 blocks (~200k rows total). Phase 1: each thread processes assigned blocks, accumulates thread-local partial hash-agg maps, merged after scan completes. Phase 2: each thread processes assigned blocks with shared read-only m map, maintains thread-local top-100 heap, heaps merged at end. Effective parallelism bounded by ~2 blocks; assign one block per thread.

indexes.notes: Read indexes/num_zonemaps.bin; for each block check uom_min <= pure_code <= uom_max; skip block if pure_code out of range. Reduces scan from 395 blocks to ~2 blocks. Applied identically to both Phase 1 and Phase 2.

decode_at_output: s.name: sub/name.bin[adsh_code] -> int32 name_code -> sub/name.dict string
